title: Falsification Test — Try to Break 'Attractor Dominates' Theory

summary.test1_weak_systems: Coupling weak systems gives mixed results — slight benefit at very low K, hurt at higher K. No clear falsification.
summary.test2_learning: Coupling helps at very short runs (100 steps, +136%) but benefit vanishes or reverses at longer runs. Transient advantage only.
summary.test3_anticorrelated: Anti-correlated coupling (K<0) gives modest benefit. Positive coupling also helps. Noisy, inconsistent — hard to draw firm conclusions.
summary.test4_exploration: Coupling to a partner in a DIFFERENT regime then uncoupling shows some benefit, especially partner ρ=55 (+24%). Suggests coupling can seed exploration.
summary.test5_network_vs_lone: EVERY network configuration beat the lone ρ=47 champion. ρ=55 and ρ=60 single agents beat it too. The combined quality of mixed networks is highest.

interpretation: The ρ=47 attractor is NOT unbeatable. Higher ρ values (55, 60) produce higher quality. Networks CAN beat single agents. However, the quality metric may scale with variance (amplitude), which grows with ρ, so this may be a measurement artifact rather than genuine creative superiority.
